findings.title: Inadequate Consent Mechanism for GDPR Compliance
findings.description: The current consent mechanism does not provide granular options for users to opt-in to specific data tracking features, which could lead to legal risks under GDPR.
assumptions: The consent mechanism is currently implemented as described.

evidence.excerpt: The current consent mechanism is inadequate for GDPR compliance, as it does not provide granular options for users to opt-in to specific data tracking features.

recommendations: Implement granular consent mechanisms that allow users to opt-in to specific data tracking features.

metadata.mitigation: Implement a tiered consent mechanism to enhance user control over data sharing.

findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit Blocker
findings.description: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch. Key concerns include lawful basis for processing behavioral data, adequacy of consent mechanisms, data retention policies, right to erasure implementation, and completion of a data protection impact assessment (DPIA).
assumptions: The system is intended for launch in the EU market.

evidence.excerpt: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch.

recommendations: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit to address key concerns.

metadata.mitigation: Complete the GDPR compliance audit and address all identified issues.

findings.title: Privacy Risks in User Profiling
findings.description: There are significant privacy risks associated with the user profiling system, particularly concerning the handling of personal data and potential re-identification of users from anonymized data.
assumptions: The system processes personal data as described.

evidence.excerpt: There are significant privacy risks associated with the user profiling system, particularly concerning the handling of personal data.

recommendations: Implement measures to enhance data anonymization and minimize re-identification risks.

metadata.mitigation: Enhance data anonymization techniques and conduct regular privacy assessments.

findings.title: Authentication Weaknesses Detected
findings.description: The authentication mechanism may have weaknesses that could be exploited, leading to unauthorized access to user data.
assumptions: The current authentication flow is as described.

evidence.excerpt: Detected auth risk keywords in 3 source(s).

recommendations: Review and strengthen the authentication mechanism to prevent unauthorized access.

metadata.mitigation: Implement multi-factor authentication and regular security audits.

findings.title: Compliance Risks Under GDPR
findings.description: The PersonaLens profiling system faces multiple compliance risks under GDPR, particularly regarding lawful basis for data processing and the right to erasure.
assumptions: The system processes personal data as described.

evidence.excerpt: The PersonaLens profiling system faces multiple compliance risks under GDPR.

recommendations: Address compliance risks by ensuring lawful basis for processing and implementing the right to erasure.

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a compliance review and implement necessary changes to align with GDPR.

summary: Identified 5 risks (2 compliance, 2 privacy, 1 security). 2 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (3 blockers, 2 warnings).
